commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces one prerequisite bullet in docs/SETUP.md to require gateway access from an existing gateway or bundled Gateway Setup path, and to say OpenClaw does not need to be pre-installed.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a documentation clarification PR rather than a runtime bug report. The source check is that current master still contains the old prerequisite text while setup/onboarding docs and code support gateway setup or existing gateway connection paths.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; this draft docs PR needs ordinary maintainer/contributor readiness and review rather than a code fix.

target.title: Clarify setup prerequisite: gateway access required, OpenClaw pre-install not required
target.body:
The setup guide prerequisite implied users must already have OpenClaw installed/accounted before installing Companion. In practice, Companion supports connecting to an existing local/remote/VPS gateway or provisioning via the bundled Gateway Setup flow.

- ### What changed
  - Updated the **Prerequisites** bullet in `docs/SETUP.md` to require only:
    - access to a gateway URL + token
    - from either an existing gateway or the bundled **OpenClaw Gateway Setup** onboarding path
  - Added explicit language that users **do not need to pre-install OpenClaw** before running the Companion installer.

- ### Why this matters
  - Removes a misleading onboarding dependency.
  - Aligns setup docs with actual supported connection paths (existing gateway or installer-assisted setup).

- ### Doc diff (representative)
```md
- An active OpenClaw account with a gateway token — sign up at openclaw.ai.
+ Access to an OpenClaw gateway URL and token (existing local/remote/VPS gateway, or one installed via OpenClaw Gateway Setup during onboarding). You do not need to pre-install OpenClaw before running this installer.
```
target.author: app/copilot-swe-agent
